---
title: "nFIESTA Docker Setup (GitLab → GitHub → Docker)"
format: html
---

# Goal

This guide shows how to:

1.  Mirror nFIESTA GitLab repos into personal GitHub
2.  Repoint submodules to GitHub
3.  Use the official Docker dev image
4.  Run the official `provision.sh` workflow

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Create workspace

```{bash}
mkdir -p ~/code/nfiesta
cd ~/code/nfiesta
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 2. Mirror GitLab repos to GitHub

## 2.1 Create empty GitHub repos

Create these repos manually on GitHub:

-   nfiesta_pg
-   nfiesta_htc
-   nfiesta_sdesign

Do NOT initialize them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2.2 Mirror nfiesta_pg

```{bash}
cd ~/code/nfiesta

git clone https://gitlab.com/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg.git
cd nfiesta_pg

git remote rename origin upstream
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/nfiesta_pg.git

git push -u origin --all
git push -u origin --tags
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2.3 Mirror nfiesta_htc

```{bash}
cd ~/code/nfiesta

git clone https://gitlab.com/nfiesta/nfiesta_htc.git
cd nfiesta_htc

git remote rename origin upstream
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/nfiesta_htc.git

git push -u origin --all
git push -u origin --tags
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2.4 Mirror nfiesta_sdesign

```{bash}
cd ~/code/nfiesta-official

git clone https://gitlab.com/nfiesta/nfiesta_sdesign.git
cd nfiesta_sdesign

git remote rename origin upstream
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/nfiesta_sdesign.git

git push -u origin --all
git push -u origin --tags
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 3. Repoint submodules to GitHub

Edit:

```{bash}
 ~/code/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg/.gitmodules
```

NOTE: show hidden files on macOS: CMD + SHIFT + .

Replace contents with:

```         
[submodule "deps/nfiesta_sdesign"]
    path = deps/nfiesta_sdesign
    url = https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/nfiesta_sdesign.git

[submodule "deps/nfiesta_htc"]
    path = deps/nfiesta_htc
    url = https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/nfiesta_htc.git
```

Commit changes:

```{bash}
cd ~/code/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg

git add .gitmodules
git commit -m "Point submodules to GitHub mirrors"
git push origin HEAD
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 4. Re-clone with submodules

```{bash}
cd ~/code/nfiesta
mv nfiesta_pg nfiesta_pg_backup

git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/nfiesta_pg.git
cd nfiesta_pg

git submodule sync
git submodule update --init --recursive
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 5. Pull official Docker image

```{bash}
docker pull registry.gitlab.com/nfiesta/nfiesta_devenv:pg16
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 6. Run container

!!! This will make a temporary container that gets deleted on exit

```{bash}
docker run --rm -it \
  --platform linux/amd64 \
  -v ~/code/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg:/builds/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg \
  -w /builds/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg \
  registry.gitlab.com/nfiesta/nfiesta_devenv:pg16 \
  bash
```

For something persistent:

-   Run a persistent nFIESTA Docker container

    ```{bash}
    docker run -it \
      --name nfiesta-dev \
      --platform linux/amd64 \
      -p 5432:5432 \
      -v ~/code/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg:/builds/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg \
      -w /builds/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg \
      registry.gitlab.com/nfiesta/nfiesta_devenv:pg16 \
      bash
    ```

<!-- -->

-   Useful commands for persistent container

    -   Exit without deleting the container

        ```{bash}
        exit
        ```

    -   Restart and attach later

        ```{bash}
        docker start -ai nfiesta-dev
        ```

        !!! IMPORTANT

        -\> May need to start postgres as well: `sudo service postgresql start`

        !!!

    -   Open a shell in the running container

        ```{bash}
        docker exec -it nfiesta-dev bash
        ```

    -   List all containers

        ```{bash}
        docker ps -a
        ```

    -   Stop the container

        ```{bash}
        docker stop nfiesta-dev
        ```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 7. Inside container

```{bash}
git config --global --add safe.directory $(pwd)
git config --global --add safe.directory $(pwd)/deps/nfiesta_htc
git config --global --add safe.directory $(pwd)/deps/nfiesta_sdesign
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 8. Run provisioning

```{bash}
bash provision.sh
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 9. Verify PostgreSQL

From inside the container run:

```{bash}
# Inside container
psql -U vagrant -d postgres
```

If out, start the container or connect to it first:

```{bash}
# Outside container
docker start -ai nfiesta-dev
# or
docker exec -it nfiesta-dev bash
```

Start the DB if needed:

```{bash}
# Inside the container
sudo service postgresql start
```

```{sql}
SELECT name, installed_version
FROM pg_available_extensions
WHERE name IN (
  'postgis',
  'plpython3u',
  'htc',
  'nfiesta_sdesign',
  'nfiesta_pg'
);
```

standard checks:

```{sql}
\l
\du
\dx
```

# Install nFIESTA extensions in a working database

## Create a clean working database for nFIESTA

```{sql}
CREATE DATABASE nfiesta_lab OWNER vagrant;
\c nfiesta_lab
```

## Create required schema

```{sql}
CREATE SCHEMA nfiesta;
```

## Install extensions

```{sql}
CREATE EXTENSION postgis;
CREATE EXTENSION plpython3u;
CREATE EXTENSION htc;
CREATE EXTENSION nfiesta_sdesign;
CREATE EXTENSION nfiesta;
```

## Verify

```{sql}
\dx
```

## Inspect schemas

```{sql}
SELECT schema_name
FROM information_schema.schemata
ORDER BY schema_name;
```

## Inspect non-system tables

```{sql}
SELECT table_schema, table_name
FROM information_schema.tables
WHERE table_schema NOT IN ('pg_catalog', 'information_schema')
ORDER BY table_schema, table_name;
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Notes

-   `pg_available_extensions` shows what **can be installed**
-   `\dx` shows what is **actually installed in the current database**
-   Extensions must be created **per database**
-   The main extension name is `nfiesta` (not `nfiesta_pg`)

# Connect to the DB from outside the container

When inside the container (typically with `docker start -ai nfiesta-dev` or `docker exec -it nfiesta-dev bash` if already running), we can connect to the DB with `psql -U vagrant -d postgres` . However from a terminal outside the container we need to allow connection to the macOS IP, which is different from 127.0.0.1:

Schema:

```         
R (Mac) → 127.0.0.1 → Docker → PostgreSQL

                        ↓ seen as 192.168.65.1
```

We need to edit `pg_hba.conf` in postgres to allow connection from that IP.

Test the connection from outside the container (start a new terminal if already in or run `exit`):

```{bash}
# Oustide container
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432 -U vagrant -d nfiesta_lab
```

If it throws an error message like this:

```{bash}
psql: error: connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5432 failed: FATAL:  no pg_hba.conf entry for host "192.168.65.1", user "vagrant", database "nfiesta_lab", SSL encryption 
connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5432 failed: FATAL:  no pg_hba.conf entry for host "192.168.65.1", user "vagrant", database "nfiesta_lab", no encryption
```

It means that we need to add the host IP "192.168.65.1" to the postgres `pg_hba.conf` file:

```{bash}
sudo nano /etc/postgresql/16/main/pg_hba.conf
```

And add this line under IPv4:

```         
host    all    all    192.168.65.1/32    md5
```

so that the file changes from this:

```         
# IPv4 local connections:
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             vagrant         172.0.0.0/8             scram-sha-256
```

to this:

```         
# IPv4 local connections:
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             vagrant         172.0.0.0/8             scram-sha-256
host    all             all             192.168.65.1/32         scram-sha-256
```

Then retest the connection from outside:

```{bash}
# Oustide container
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432 -U vagrant -d nfiesta_lab
```

# Run nFIESTA minimal working example

## Get nfiesta_gui

nFIESTA GUI releases are in: <https://gitlab.com/nfiesta/nfiesta_gui/-/releases>

Install nfiesta_gui in windows or windows container (e.g. Parallel desktop). In Parallel desktop, installation works well, but IP of the server has to direct to Mac IP (not DB IP or docker IP):

Run in macos terminal:

```{bash}
ipconfig getifaddr en0 

# example result
# > 192.168.1.63
```

Test from Windows terminal:

```{bash}
ping 192.168.1.63
```

In nfiesta_gui, use this IP as server:

```         
Server: 192.168.1.63
Port: 5432
Database: nfiesta_lab
User: vagrant
Password: ****
```

## Get example data

Get the data into the DB, following these instructions: <https://gitlab.com/nfiesta/pathfinder_demo_study/-/wikis/CSV-files-quality-control>

First need to install support for large file in git:

```{bash}
brew install git-lfs

# Then initialize
git lfs install

# Check version
# git lfs version
```

Clone the gitlab repo:

```{bash}
cd ~/code/nfiesta

git clone https://gitlab.com/nfiesta/pathfinder_demo_study.git

cd pathfinder_demo_study
```

Create a new directory with the CSV data from csv_import/countries/example_data_2_0 into csv_import/countries/Czechia:

```{bash}
cd csv_import/countries/

mkdir Czechia

cp example_data_2_0/*csv Czechia/
```

Check data in place

```{bash}
ls Czechia
```

Next step is to create the docker container with the data. This step takes a long time as it sets up the container with the database and import the CSV into it and run tests.

NOTE: edit `docker-compose.yml`

-   On ARM machine it may be necessary to and replace `platform: linux` with `platform: linux/amd64`

-   If port 5432 is already used port can be edited in the same file. Te get to port 5433 for example, replace `${NFIESTA_PORT:-5432}:5432` with `${NFIESTA_PORT:-5433}:5432` or another number (on the left side only).

```{bash}
# If needed
# cd csv_import/
NFIESTA_COUNTRY='["CZ"]' COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME='cz' docker compose up
```

Once installed update the following extensions: `nfiesta` and `nfiesta-sdesign`

```{bash}
# Stop/start copntainer if needed
# docker stop pf_db_cz
# docker start pf_db_cz

# Connect to the container
docker exec -it pf_db_cz bash

# Connect to the DB
psql -d pathfinder_ds
```

Then in SQL:

```{sql}
ALTER EXTENSION nfiesta UPDATE TO '4.2.1';
ALTER EXTENSION nfiesta_sdesign UPDATE TO '1.1.17';
\dx
```

## Modifying the data to get the spatial information into place

Database Structure:

![](https://gitlab.com/nfiesta/nfiesta_pg/-/wikis/myuploads/erd/nfi_esta_yEd.png)

### Create the NUTS boundaries from the INSPIRE 1 km grid

Objective: create estimation cells with NUTS (EU regions and admin boundaries) from INSPIRE 1 km grid

Target file: `pathfinder_demo_study/csv_import/provisioning/provision_cells_use4estimates_nuts.sql`

This file reconfigure the admin for the target countries and its direct neighbours. At the time of the study, the country in the file was Finland (FI) with neighbour Norway and Sweden

Task:

-   Change the target country to CZ and remove neighbours:

    -   change 'FI' with 'CZ' (9 occurences)

    -   change 'NO', 'SE' to '' -\> removing neighbour

-   Run the sql script from within csv_import in the container:

```{bash}
# If needed
# cd pathfinder_demo_study/csv_import 
docker exec -it pf_db_cz bash
psql -d pathfinder_ds -f ./provisioning/provision_cells_use4estimates_nuts.sql
```

### Add stratum to the estimation cells

Objective: Populate a table in NFiesta schema: t_stratum_in_estimation_cell

Task: Run `stratum2estimation_cell_artifacts.py` from within csv_import in the docker container

```{bash}
# If needed
# cd pathfinder_demo_study/csv_import 
# docker exec -it pf_db_cz bash
python3 ./provisioning/nfiesta/stratum2estimation_cell_artifacts.py --connectionstring='dbname=pathfinder_ds' --country=CZ --processes=60 --insert
```

Optional: Check in QGIS that the NUTS cells are for the correct lcoation

In QGIS go to Open \> PostreSQL, write the connection parameter (adapt to your DB params):

```         
Name: nfiesta-CZ 
Host: localhost 
Port: 5452 
Database: pathfinder_ds 
Authentication (Basic): 
  User Name: vagrant 
  Password: \*\*\*\*
```
